title: docs: add `markdown-it-image-size` to reduce layout shift
author: sapphi-red

dependency_context_notes: Mentions `create-vite-app` command, Vetur validator, and Vue component structure but no file paths or specific module references
behavioral_specificity_notes: Some concrete issues shown (404 links, missing aria labels, lazy loading) but limited detail on implementation approach

analysis.outcome_clarity_notes: PARTIALLY PRESENT. The linked issue clearly describes problems (Vetur complaints, multiple root elements, type-checking issues) but the PR description lists fixes without connecting to the issue or specifying what was actually changed. Two developers would struggle to know if all aspects of the issue were addressed.
analysis.error_states_notes: ABSENT. No error handling, edge cases, or failure modes discussed.
analysis.scope_boundaries_notes: PARTIALLY PRESENT. The linked issue defines a specific problem (App.vue template), but the PR description appears to fix unrelated doc issues (avatar links, aria labels, lazy loading). The relationship between the issue and PR is unclear—scope seems to have drifted.
analysis.acceptance_criteria_notes: MINIMAL. No explicit pass/fail criteria. Visual examples in the issue show problems but no test cases or specific acceptance thresholds are provided.
analysis.data_contracts_notes: ABSENT. This is documentation/template work, not a data-related change.
analysis.dependency_context_notes: MINIMAL. References Vetur, Vue 3 setup(), `create-vite-app`, but no file paths, module boundaries, or integration points specified.
analysis.behavioral_specificity_notes: PARTIALLY PRESENT. Issue shows specific problems with screenshots; PR lists concrete fixes (404 links, aria labels, lazy loading attributes) but lacks quantification or detailed acceptance criteria.
analysis.mismatch_concern: The linked issue requests fixes to App.vue template (defineComponent, root element wrapper, data() form), but the PR description describes doc updates (image links, aria labels, lazy loading). These appear to be different work items.
